rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE CHUNG HOP WONG AND KING TSUI LAU WONG REVOCABL TRUST dated sep 21, 2009 amended Sep 26 2009
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents In Responseto Petitioners Request For Production Of Documents Or Things, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Petitioners' Motion to Compel Production of Documents in Response to Petitioners' Request for Production of Documents or Things, Set Two ("Motion"). The Motion is DENIED as procedurally defective. Per the Notice of Motion, the Motion is made pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 2025.450 and 2025.480. These Code sections pertain to a deposed party's failure to appear at a deposition or to bring any document, electronically stored information, or tangible thing described in the deposition notice. The substance of the Motion, however, pertains to compelling a response to a Request for Production of Documents, which is a form of discovery governed by Code of Civil Procedure sections 2031 through 2031.510. The court directs both attorneys of record to familiarize themselves with the Discovery Act and requirements thereto. Recent filings by both Mr. Hales and Mr. Malachowski have evidenced glaringly inadequate work product, to wit, procedurally defective subpoenas, multiple errata to filings and supplemental declarations in support of motions, cross-allegations of failure to meet and confer in good faith, and now this defective Motion answered by an opposition that does not address, and presumably did not realize, the fatal defect in notice at all. Counsel is advised that continued waste of judicial resources via defective or erroneous filings, by either attorney, will draw sanctions from the court. Respondent shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
